dissent_flag.dissent_reason: Macro agent flagged VIX rising +9.43% into HIGH regime (26.45) as the dominant risk-off headwind — specifically noting that 'VIX spike typically dominates in risk-off regimes' and the overnight gap (+0.15%) is too small relative to the fear move to represent institutional conviction.

validator_assessment.weakest_link: The overnight gap of only +0.15% is being treated as bullish conviction when it is undersized relative to the +9.43% VIX spike — classic gap-and-fade signature where overnight buyers may be trapped above 659.72.
validator_assessment.top_invalidation_scenario: SPY fails to hold 660.37 on first RTH test and breaks below 659.72 within the first 90 minutes, triggering ribbon inversion and confirming the bear-trap setup.

primary.condition: If SPY breaks and closes above 660.37 on first RTH test with body >= 10c above the level

secondary.condition: If SPY holds above 659.72 but repeatedly rejects at 660.37 without a clean close above

invalidation.condition: SPY fails 660.37 on first test and breaks below 659.72 within first 90 minutes of RTH, confirmed by ribbon inversion (price closes below 659.30)
invalidation.effect: Bull thesis invalidated — gap-and-fade confirmed. Cascade through 659.0 and 658.0 support likely as VIX buyers dominate and ribbon inverts. Pivot to bearish bias if this scenario triggers.

swarm_predictions.claim: 660.37 (premarket high / battle level) is tested from below within the first 60 minutes of RTH (before 10:30 ET)
swarm_predictions.invalidation: SPY does not touch 660.37 before 10:30 ET, instead consolidating below 659.72 or pulling back toward 659.0

swarm_predictions.claim: VIX exceeds 27.00 at some point within the first 2 hours of RTH (before 11:30 ET), escalating the risk-off bid
swarm_predictions.invalidation: VIX remains below 27.00 through 11:30 ET, suggesting the +9.43% overnight VIX spike was a pre-market overreaction that fades at open

swarm_predictions.claim: SPY opens at or above 659.72 and the CLEAN_BULL ribbon (fast 659.69 / pivot 659.47 / slow 658.81) remains intact through the first 30 minutes of RTH, with price not closing below the ribbon inversion threshold of 659.30
swarm_predictions.invalidation: SPY prints a 5-minute bar close below 659.30 before 10:00 ET, inverting the ribbon and negating the technical bull structure

synthesis_narrative: Today's setup is a contested high-volatility market: 3 of 4 specialists favor bulls on the basis of a CLEAN_BULL ribbon (88c spread), an overnight gap up to 659.83, and price already above prior RTH high 659.72 — but the macro agent and validator both flag VIX rising +9.43% into 26.45 HIGH regime as the dominant risk-off headwind that historically overwhelms undersized gap-up technicals (the gap is only +0.15%, dwarfed by the VIX move). The single most critical watch at market open is the 660.37 battle level: a decisive body-close above it on first test confirms institutional buying and opens the bull path to 661.0; a rejection at 660.37 followed by a break below 659.72 confirms the validator's gap-and-fade scenario and triggers the bearish cascade through 659.0 and 658.0.

technical.one_line: CLEAN_BULL ribbon (88c spread, price 36c above pivot) + overnight gap up 96c; mixed bar structure with last bar a pullback candle — cautious bullish

macro.one_line: VIX 26.45 rising +9.43% into HIGH regime with RISK_OFF backdrop; no events today but macro fear dominates; bearish bias on contested setup

level_thesis.one_line: Battle level 660.37 (PMH resistance) from below; price above 659.72 prior RTH high; primary scenario = break above 660.37 targets 661.0

internals.one_line: XLF +0.75% + XLE +0.96% cyclical/reflation rotation confirmed; narrow breadth (XLK +0.22% lagging) limits upside sustainability

validator.top_invalidation: Gap-and-fade setup: +0.15% overnight gap is too small vs +9.43% VIX spike; ribbon 53c away from inversion; XLK flat signals narrow breadth ceiling
